sources: CMS sample MSNs, BCBS/Aetna/UHC EOBs, Mayo Clinic, SSM Health, HuggingFace medical_bills dataset, Virginia Riverside

glm_ocr_0.9B.notes: Model extracts minimal structure from real documents. Most line items empty or $0.00.

gpt_5.4.notes: Extracts significantly more content but still only 25% code extraction rate on real documents.

key_finding: The gap between synthetic and real document performance is massive for ALL models. GLM-OCR drops from 86.4 (synthetic) to near-zero (real). GPT-5.4 maintains extraction of amounts/totals but CPT code extraction drops to 25%. Real medical bills with diverse layouts, mixed fonts, revenue codes, and complex formatting remain an unsolved challenge for current VLMs.

synthetic_vs_real.gpt54_real_effective: ~40-50 (estimated from 25% code rate, good amount extraction)

implication: Synthetic benchmarks dramatically overestimate real-world VLM performance on medical documents. The synthetic-to-real gap may be the most important finding in this study.
